description.pass1_value: Airtable is a cloud spreadsheet-database hybrid for building collaborative apps and workflows with a REST API, SDKs, webhooks, and OAuth integration.
description.final_value: Airtable is an app-building platform for creating and sharing business applications.

pass1_value.note: Official docs state Airtable supports OAuth 2.0 and Personal access tokens (used as Bearer tokens). The docs also reference legacy API key usage; present auth methods are OAuth and personal access tokens (Bearer). 'other' included to reflect legacy API key mention in docs.

final_value.note: Airtable Web API authentication documentation states the API uses personal access tokens (sent as a Bearer token) or OAuth 2.0.

pass1_value.note: The support article documents how a user creates personal access tokens from their account settings; the public pricing page shows a Free plan, and the getting-started guide links to token creation—indicating developers can obtain tokens via self-serve without human approval.

final_value.note: Official support documentation explains how users create personal access tokens from their Airtable account (self-serve).

pass1_value.note: Public REST docs and self-service personal access tokens (or OAuth app registration) are available, so an integration/agent can be developed today.

final_value.note: Airtable provides public REST API documentation and supports authentication via self-serve personal access tokens or OAuth 2.0, so an integration can be built today.
